close_reason: Completed all 16 subtasks: Buck2 rules, deps management, module integration, and examples
description:
Add Buck2 rules for compiling Solidity smart contracts.

## Background
Solidity is the primary language for Ethereum smart contracts. Compilation produces:
- ABI (Application Binary Interface) - JSON describing contract interface
- Bytecode - EVM bytecode for deployment
- Source maps - for debugging

## Desired Rules

### solidity_library
Compile Solidity contracts, exposing artifacts for other rules.
```starlark
solidity_library(
    name = "token",
    srcs = ["ERC20.sol", "MyToken.sol"],
    deps = [":openzeppelin"],  # Other solidity_library targets
    remappings = {
        "@openzeppelin/": "//third_party/openzeppelin/",
    },
    solc_version = "0.8.20",  # Optional, use toolchain default
    optimizer = True,
    optimizer_runs = 200,
)
```

### solidity_contract (or solidity_binary)
Build a deployable contract with all artifacts.
```starlark
solidity_contract(
    name = "my_token",
    contract = "MyToken",  # Contract name within sources
    deps = [":token"],
    visibility = ["PUBLIC"],
)
```

Outputs:
- `my_token.abi` - Contract ABI (JSON)
- `my_token.bin` - Bytecode (hex)
- `my_token.metadata` - Compiler metadata

### solidity_test (future)
Integration with testing frameworks like Foundry or Hardhat.

## Toolchain
- Add `solc` (Solidity compiler) to registry
- nixpkgs has `solc` package
- Consider supporting multiple solc versions (important for contract compatibility)
- May want `foundry` toolchain for testing (forge, cast, anvil)

## Implementation Notes
- Solidity has complex import resolution (remappings, node_modules style)
- Standard JSON input/output for compiler interface
- Need to handle contract inheritance and libraries
- Consider integration with OpenZeppelin and other common libraries
- Source verification requires exact compiler settings

## Third-party Dependencies
Consider how to handle:
- OpenZeppelin contracts
- Other audited contract libraries
- npm-style Solidity packages

## References
- https://docs.soliditylang.org/
- https://github.com/ethereum/solidity
- https://book.getfoundry.sh/ (Foundry)
title: Add Solidity rules to prelude extensions
